content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration        Executive/Legal        Enforcement        Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                            322-5901              322-6441           322-6444

January 11, 1978

Margery Phelan
Deputy City Clerk
City of San Gabriel
P.O. Box 130
San Gabriel, CA  91778

Dear Ms. Phelan:

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has received
your letter dated December 28, 1977, concerning Opinion
Request No. 76-025, which asked what campaign statement was
to be filed by an elected officer who resigned from office.

     On October 5, 1976, the Commission adopted its
permanent regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18426 (a copy
of which is enclosed), clarifying the reporting obligations
for semiannual campaign statements. Under that regulation,
a semiannual campaign statement can be filed any time prior
to the closing date as long as the statement includes all
expenditures and contributions made or received during the
period covered.

     Therefore to address the situation you posed of a
councilmember who is not seeking reelection, the councilmember,
as an official, is required under Government Code Section
84206, to file a semiannual campaign statement, whether or
not contributions or expenditures have been made or received
during the period covered. The semiannual campaign statement
must be filed no later than July 31, 1978, covering the
period from January 1 through June 30, 1978. Under 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18426, the councilmember's semiannual
campaign statement may be filed at any time prior to July 31,
1978, as long as that campaign statement contains all con-
tributions or expenditures received or made or expected to
be made through June 30, 1978. If the statement is filed
early and additional contributions or expenditures are sub-
sequently received or made, the semiannual campaign state-
ment should be amended.

Margery Phelan
January 11, 1978
Page Two


I hope this information is helpful to you. If you
have any other questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,

Barbara Campbell
Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

BC:plh
Enclosure

CITY OF SAN GABRIEL
FOUNDED 1771
INCORPORATED 1913

532 WEST MISSION DRIVE • SAN GABRIEL, CALIFORNIA 91776 • (213) 282-4104 • 283-2591
P.O. BOX 130 • SAN GABRIEL, CALIFORNIA 91778

ROBERT D. JONES
CITY CLERK

CITY WITH A MISSION

December 28, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

We received a letter from you dated March 8, 1976, Re:
Request for Opinion No. 76-025.

In this letter you stated that the Commission had
determined that the issue raised should be addressed by
a regulation of general applicability instead of by an
opinion. It was to be heard at your May 1976 Commission
Meeting. We did not receive any word as to the outcome
of the hearing. The question asked was whether a Council
Member who did not seek reelection and who resigned a
month before his term expired needed to file a Campaign
Statement for the period from the beginning of the year
until he resigned.

At this time we have a Council Member who is not
seeking reelection. What is the date his Campaign State-
ment will be due.

Yours truly,

Margery H. Phelan
Deputy City Clerk

sections.question: What is the date a Campaign Statement will be due for a Council Member who is not seeking reelection?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of San Gabriel seeks clarification on when a Council Member who is not seeking reelection must file a Campaign Statement, following up on a prior opinion request (76-025) regarding whether such a statement is required for the period prior to resignation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is heavily degraded/poorly scanned with significant OCR errors and missing text. Identified as incoming request rather than FPPC advice letter based on letterhead from City of San Gabriel to FPPC, requesting guidance rather than providing it. FPPC response appears to be on reverse side but is illegible. No clear CONCLUSION section found in readable text.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of San Gabriel City Clerk dated December 28, 1977, following up on a prior FPPC opinion request (76-025) from March 1976 regarding Campaign Statement filing requirements for a Council Member not seeking reelection, and asking for clarification on the due date for such a statement.
